title: Contributor Covenant Code of Conduct
text: members, contributors, leaders pledge make participation community harassment-free experience everyone, regardless age, body size, visible invisible disability, ethnicity, sex characteristics, gender identity expression, level experience, education, socio-economic status, nationality, personal appearance, race, caste, color, religion, sexual identity orientation. pledge act interact ways contribute open, welcoming, diverse, inclusive, healthy community.

title: Contributor Covenant Code of Conduct
text: Examples behavior contributes positive environment community include: Demonstrating empathy kindness toward people respectful differing opinions, viewpoints, experiences Giving gracefully accepting constructive feedback Accepting responsibility apologizing affected mistakes, learning experience Focusing best just us individuals, overall community Examples unacceptable behavior include: use sexualized language imagery, sexual attention advances kind Trolling, insulting derogatory comments, personal political attacks Public private harassment Publishing others’ private information, physical email address, without explicit permission conduct reasonably considered inappropriate professional setting

title: Contributor Covenant Code of Conduct
text: Community leaders responsible clarifying enforcing standards acceptable behavior take appropriate fair corrective action response behavior deem inappropriate, threatening, offensive, harmful. Community leaders right responsibility remove, edit, reject comments, commits, code, wiki edits, issues, contributions aligned Code Conduct, communicate reasons moderation decisions appropriate.

title: Contributor Covenant Code of Conduct
text: Code Conduct applies within community spaces, also applies individual officially representing community public spaces. Examples representing community include using official e-mail address, posting via official social media account, acting appointed representative online offline event.

title: Contributor Covenant Code of Conduct
text: Instances abusive, harassing, otherwise unacceptable behavior may reported community leaders responsible enforcement rdev.nc@gmail.com. complaints reviewed investigated promptly fairly. community leaders obligated respect privacy security reporter incident.

title: Contributor Covenant Code of Conduct
text: Community leaders follow Community Impact Guidelines determining consequences action deem violation Code Conduct:

title: Contributor Covenant Code of Conduct
text: Community Impact: Use inappropriate language behavior deemed unprofessional unwelcome community. Consequence: private, written warning community leaders, providing clarity around nature violation explanation behavior inappropriate. public apology may requested.

title: Contributor Covenant Code of Conduct
text: Community Impact: violation single incident series actions. Consequence: warning consequences continued behavior. interaction people involved, including unsolicited interaction enforcing Code Conduct, specified period time. includes avoiding interactions community spaces well external channels like social media. Violating terms may lead temporary permanent ban.

title: Contributor Covenant Code of Conduct
text: Community Impact: serious violation community standards, including sustained inappropriate behavior. Consequence: temporary ban sort interaction public communication community specified period time. public private interaction people involved, including unsolicited interaction enforcing Code Conduct, allowed period. Violating terms may lead permanent ban.

title: Contributor Covenant Code of Conduct
text: Community Impact: Demonstrating pattern violation community standards, including sustained inappropriate behavior, harassment individual, aggression toward disparagement classes individuals. Consequence: permanent ban sort public interaction within community.

title: Contributor Covenant Code of Conduct
text: Code Conduct adapted Contributor Covenant, version 2.1, available https://www.contributor-covenant.org/version/2/1/code_of_conduct.html. Community Impact Guidelines inspired Mozilla’s code conduct enforcement ladder. answers common questions code conduct, see FAQ https://www.contributor-covenant.org/faq. Translations available https://www.contributor-covenant.org/translations.

text: Code conduct Style guide Commit messages Asking questions Reporting bugs Requesting features Contributing code

text: Please note package released Contributor Code Conduct. contributing project, agree abide terms.

title: Contributing to fairpub
text: use Tidyverse style guide writing R code. can install air automatically format R code. Functions documented roxygen2 syntax. fairpub uses lower_snake_case.

title: Contributing to fairpub
text: want contribute commiting changes, please try use Conventional commits specification.

title: Contributing to fairpub
text: ask question, best search existing Issues might help . case found suitable issue still need clarification, can write question issue. still feel need ask question need clarification, recommend following: Open new Issue. Use template other_issue.md. Provide much context can ’re running . Provide project platform versions (paste output sessionInfo()). take care issue soon possible.

title: Contributing to fairpub
text: good bug report shouldn’t leave others needing chase information. Therefore, ask investigate carefully, collect information describe issue detail report. Please complete following steps advance help us fix potential bug fast possible. Make sure using latest version fairpub. Determine bug really bug error side. see users experienced (potentially already solved) issue , check already bug report existing bug error bug tracker.

what: How do I submit a bug report?
title: Contributing to fairpub
text: use GitHub Issues track bugs errors. run issue project: Open new Issue. Use template bug_report.md. Explain behavior expect actual behavior. Please provide much context possible describe reproduction steps someone else can follow recreate issue . usually includes code reproducible example. take care issue soon possible.

title: Contributing to fairpub
text: Make sure using latest version fairpub. Read documentation carefully find functionality already covered. Perform search see enhancement already suggested. , add comment existing issue instead opening new one.

what: How do I submit a feature request?
title: Contributing to fairpub
text: Feature requests tracked GitHub Issues. Open new Issue. Use template feature_request.md. Provide clear descriptive title issue identify suggestion. Provide step--step description suggested enhancement many details possible. Explain enhancement useful fairpub users. take care issue soon possible.

title: Contributing to fairpub
text: use GitHub flow collaborate project: Fork repository using GitHub interface. Clone fork using git clone fork-url (replace fork-url URL fork). Alternatively, open RStudio IDE Positron IDE create New Project Version Control. Create new branch w/ git checkout -b branch-name (replace branch-name name new branch). Make contribution (see examples). Stage (git add) commit (git commit) changes often necessary Push changes GitHub w/ git push origin branch-name. Submit Pull Request original repo. review PR soon possible.

title: Contributing to fairpub
text: want contribute improving README, please edit README.Rmd (README.md). forget update README.md running:
code: rmarkdown::render("README.Rmd")

title: Contributing to fairpub
text: want contribute editing existing vignette, just edit corresponding Rmd file stored vignettes/ folder. want contribute adding new vignette, create new Rmd file vignettes/ folder add following header: use new external dependency, forget add DESCRIPTION file section Suggests (package already listed section Imports). Check integrity package :
code: --- title: "Vignette Title" output: rmarkdown::html_vignette vignette: >   %\VignetteIndexEntry{Vignette Title}   %\VignetteEngine{knitr::rmarkdown}   %\VignetteEncoding{UTF-8} --- devtools::check()

title: Contributing to fairpub
text: want contribute improving documentation function, open corresponding file R/ folder edit lines starting #' (roxygen2 syntax). Update documentation (Rd files man/ folder) running: use new external dependency example section, forget add DESCRIPTION file section Imports (package already listed). Check integrity package :
code: devtools::document() devtools::check()

title: Contributing to fairpub
text: want contribute improving code function, open edit corresponding file R/ folder. Check integrity package : forget adapt unit tests function editing corresponding file stored tests/testthat/ folder. use package testthat implement unit tests. Check tests running:
code: devtools::check() devtools::test()

title: Contributing to fairpub
text: want contribute submitting new feature, please follow workflow: Create new R file folder R/. Implement code function. Document function w/ roxygen2 syntax. necessary, add additional dependencies DESCRIPTION file. Update package documentation w/ devtools::document(). Create new R file folder tests/testthat/. Implement unit tests new function. Check integrity package w/ devtools::check(). Thanks contribution!

title: Get started
text: Scientific journals operate broad spectrum publishing strategies, strictly -profit, non-profit, -business models (e.g. -profit academic friendly journals). Scientific publishing increasingly dominated -profit journals, many attract prestige submissions high impact factors (McGill 2024). contrast, non-profit journals – reinvest revenue academic community – struggle maintain visibility despite offering equitable publishing models. R package fairpub aims provide user-friendly toolbox investigate fairness research (article, bibliographic list, citation list, etc.). fairness measured according two dimensions: Business model journal: -profit vs. non-profit Academic friendly status journal: yes journal non-profit business model fairer academic friendly journal -profit business model. later still fairer non-academic friendly journal -profit business model. information comes DAFNEE database, Database Academia Friendly jourNals Ecology Evolution. Note: package fairpub provides subset original DAFNEE database (fields “Ecology”, “Evolution/Systematics”, “General” “Organisms”). currently working increase list journals. package fairpub also implements method proposed Beck et al. (2026): strategic citation. deliberately choosing cite relevant articles non-profit journals multiple references equally valid, researchers can contribute increasing visibility future impact factor. method implemented fp_compute_citation_ratio() function can answer question fair cite previous works? computing fairness ratio references cited manuscript. fp_compute_citation_ratio() function can also used answer question fair publication list? computing fairness ratio bibliography author.

title: Get started
text: Let’s explore features fairpub first let’s load attach fairpub package: functions fairpub query OpenAlex bibliographic database using openalexR package. recommended OpenAlex API documentation, ’s good practice send email address query made openalexR. Use function options() store email address global variable available current session: Note: registration required OpenAlex website.
code: library(fairpub) options(openalexR.mailto = 'your_email@mail.com')

title: Get started
text: function fp_get_journal_fairness() package fairpub queries DAFNEE database retrieve fairness journal. function returns two-column data.frame name journal fairness status journal. fairness status can following three values: Non-profit academic friendly -profit academic friendly -profit non-academic friendly function searches exact match (case insensitive) journal found, fuzzy search returns three best candidates (low distance characters). instance: , user can run function correct spelling: Note: mentioned , list DAFNEE journals included fairpub exhaustive, ’s possible many journals missing.
code: fp_get_journal_fairness(journal = "Science") #>   journal                         fairness #> 1 Science Non-profit and academic friendly fp_get_journal_fairness(journal = "Science of Nature") #> No exact match found! #> The fuzzy search returns these three best candidates: #>   'The Science of Nature' #>   'Science Advances' #>   'People and Nature' fp_get_journal_fairness(journal = "The Science of Nature") #>                 journal                             fairness #> 1 The Science of Nature For-profit and non-academic friendly

title: Get started
text: function fp_get_article_fairness() package fairpub dedicated retrieve fairness scientific article. function first queries OpenAlex bibliographic database find journal name DOI (digital object identifier) article. , journal name used query DAFNEE database get fairness status. Note: even function works article level, fairness status associated journal. words, articles published journal fairness status. output identical output fp_get_journal_fairness(), .e. two-column data.frame. Note: article found OpenAlex, output : Note: article found OpenAlex journal absent DAFNEE, output :
code: fp_get_article_fairness(doi = "10.1126/science.162.3859.1243") #>   journal                         fairness #> 1 Science Non-profit and academic friendly fp_get_article_fairness(doi = "10.xxxx/xxxx") #>   journal                     fairness #> 1      NA Record not found in OpenAlex fp_get_article_fairness(doi = "10.21105/joss.05753") #>                               journal                            fairness #> 1 The Journal of Open Source Software Record not found in DAFNEE database

title: Get started
text: One core function fairpub fp_compute_citation_ratio(). computes fairness ratio list references cited manuscript. ratio can used author replace -profit citations fairer citations (e.g.  citations articles published non-profit journals). course, findings two studies similar. function uses one argument: vector DOI. fairpub package provides fp_extract_doi() function reads BibTeX file extracts available DOI. Let’s use function fp_extract_doi() BibTeX file provided example fairpub. Note: make sure check references, especially DOI field. example, references don’t DOI (book, book chapter, etc.). fp_extract_doi() function don’t remove references provide full report (see output fp_compute_citation_ratio() function). Now vector DOI, can use fp_compute_citation_ratio() function compute fairness ratio list. function returns two-element list: summary: two-column data.frame number references remaining step workflow ratio: three-element vector value three fairness ratios example, list references fairness ratio (Non-profit academic friendly) 82%. value must interpreted caution. Indeed ratio computed 29% (11 38) references, journal 27 articles indexed DAFNEE database. Note: mentioned , currently working improve list journals indexed DAFNEE database.
code: # Path to the BibTeX provided by 'fairpub' ---- filename <- system.file(   file.path("extdata", "references.bib"),   package = "fairpub" )  # Extract DOI from this BibTeX file ---- doi_list <- fp_extract_doi(file = filename) doi_list #>  [1] "10.1098/rsos.160384"               NA                                  #>  [3] "10.1126/science.1212540"           "10.9745/ghsp-d-21-00145"           #>  [5] "10.1126/science.adk9900"           "10.1016/j.ecolecon.2021.107082"    #>  [7] "10.1177/014107680609900316"        "10.1093/reseval/rvad012"           #>  [9] "10.1111/ele.14395"                 "10.3998/ptpbio.3363"               #> [11] "10.4000/proceedings.elpub.2018.30" "10.1093/scipol/scs093"             #> [13] NA                                  "10.1002/leap.1102"                 #> [15] "10.1087/0953151053584975"          NA                                  #> [17] "10.1177/0263395719858571"          "10.1017/s1062798709000532"         #> [19] "10.1371/journal.pone.0127502"      "10.1162/qss_c_00305"               #> [21] "10.1007/978-3-030-02511-3_1"       NA                                  #> [23] "10.1371/journal.pbio.1002264"      "10.3389/frma.2016.00007"           #> [25] "10.48550/arxiv.2407.16551"         NA                                  #> [27] "10.1080/08109028.2014.891710"      "10.1007/s11192-022-04586-1"        #> [29] "10.1098/rspb.2019.2047"            "10.5281/zenodo.4558704"            #> [31] "10.1162/qss_a_00272"               "10.3917/inno.063.0095"             #> [33] "10.1371/journal.pone.0243664"      "10.1257/jep.15.4.183"              #> [35] "10.1073/pnas.0305628101"           "10.1016/j.amjmed.2019.07.028"      #> [37] "10.32614/rj-2023-089"              "10.5534/wjmh.230001" fp_compute_citation_ratio(doi = doi_list) #> $summary #>                                            metric value #> 1                                Total references    38 #> 2                             References with DOI    33 #> 3                         Deduplicated references    33 #> 4                    References found in OpenAlex    33 #> 5                      References found in DAFNEE    11 #> 6     Non-profit and academic friendly references     9 #> 7     For-profit and academic friendly references     2 #> 8 For-profit and non-academic friendly references     0 #> #> $ratios #>     Non-profit and academic friendly     For-profit and academic friendly #>                                 0.82                                 0.18 #> For-profit and non-academic friendly #>                                 0.00

Scientific publishing increasingly dominated -profit journals, many attract prestige submissions high impact factors (McGill 2024). contrast, non-profit journals – reinvest revenue academic community – struggle maintain visibility despite offering equitable publishing models. R package fairpub aims provide user-friendly toolbox investigate fairness research (article, bibliographic list, citation list, etc.). fairness measured according two dimensions: business model journal: -profit vs. non-profit academic friendly status journal: yes journal non-profit business model fairer academic friendly journal -profit business model. later still fairer non-academic friendly journal -profit business model. information comes DAFNEE initiative, Database Academia Friendly jourNals Ecology Evolution. package fairpub also implements method proposed Beck et al. text: fairpub package can: retrieve fairness status journal fp_get_journal_fairness() function retrieve fairness status article fp_get_article_fairness() function querying OpenAlex bibliographic database compute fairness ratio list references cited manuscript fp_compute_citation_ratio() function compute fairness ratio publications author (team) fp_compute_citation_ratio() function addition, fp_extract_doi() function helps user easily extract DOI BibTeX file. list DOI can pass fp_compute_citation_ratio() function.

code: # Install < remotes > package (if not already installed) ---- if (!requireNamespace("remotes", quietly = TRUE)) {   install.packages("remotes") }  # Install < fairpub > from GitHub ---- remotes::install_github("frbcesab/fairpub") library("fairpub")

title: How Fair Are You When You Publish/Cite Scientific Works?
text: main function fairpub fp_compute_citation_ratio(). vector article DOI, function report following metrics: example, list references fairness ratio (Non-profit academic friendly) 90%. value must interpreted caution. Indeed ratio computed 26% (10 38) references, journal 20 articles indexed DAFNEE database. Visit Get started vignette complete usage fairpub package.
code: fp_compute_citation_ratio(doi = list_of_doi) ## $summary ##                                     metric value ##                           Total references    33 ##                        References with DOI    33 ##                    Deduplicated references    33 ##               References found in OpenAlex    30 ##                 References found in DAFNEE    10 ##     Non-profit & acad. friendly references     9 ##     For-profit & acad. friendly references     1 ## For-profit & non-acad. friendly references     0 ##  ## $ratios ## Non-profit & acad. friendly  For-profit & acad. friendly  For-profit & non-acad. friendly  ##                         0.9                          0.1                              0.0

title: Clean a DOI vector — fp_clean_doi
text: helper cleans DOIs (Digital Object Identifier) removing prefix (doi:, https://doi.org/ http://dx.doi.org/) using lower case.

title: Clean a DOI vector — fp_clean_doi
code: fp_clean_doi(doi = NULL)

title: Clean a DOI vector — fp_clean_doi
text: doi character vector Digital Object Identifiers (DOI).

title: Clean a DOI vector — fp_clean_doi
text: character DOI without prefix lower case.

what: Non profit & academic friendly ratio of citations — fp_compute_citation_ratio
title: Non profit & academic friendly ratio of citations — fp_compute_citation_ratio
text: Scientific journals operate broad spectrum publishing strategies, strictly -profit, non-profit, -business models (e.g. -profit academic friendly journals). list references, function computes three citation ratios: proportion non-profit citations, proportion -profit academic friendly citations, proportion -profit non-academic friendly citations (Beck et al. 2026). uses OpenAlex bibliographic database (https://openalex.org) retrieve journal names article DOI DAFNEE database (https://dafnee.isem-evolution.fr/) get business model academic friendly status journals.

title: Non profit & academic friendly ratio of citations — fp_compute_citation_ratio
code: fp_compute_citation_ratio(doi = NULL)

title: Non profit & academic friendly ratio of citations — fp_compute_citation_ratio
text: doi character vector Digital Object Identifiers (DOI). Can contain NA (book, book chapter, etc.).

title: Non profit & academic friendly ratio of citations — fp_compute_citation_ratio
text: list two elements: summary, data.frame two columns (metric value) reporting following statistics: number total references (length doi argument) number references DOI number deduplicated references number references found OpenAlex database number references whose journal indexed DAFNEE database number non-profit academic friendly references number -profit academic friendly references number -profit non academic friendly references ratios, vector three ratios: non-profit academic friendly ratio -profit academic friendly ratio -profit non academic friendly ratio

title: Non profit & academic friendly ratio of citations — fp_compute_citation_ratio
text: Beck M et al. (2026) Citation self-awareness fairer academic publishing landscape. BioScience. DOI: doi:10.1093/biosci/biag028

title: Non profit & academic friendly ratio of citations — fp_compute_citation_ratio
code: # Be polite and send your email to OpenAlex API ---- options(openalexR.mailto = 'anonymous@mail.com')  # Path to the BibTeX provided by <fairpub> ---- filename <- system.file(   file.path("extdata", "references.bib"),   package = "fairpub" )  # Extract DOI from BibTeX ---- doi_list <- fp_extract_doi(file = filename)  # Print DOI ---- head(doi_list) #> [1] "10.1098/rsos.160384"            NA                               #> [3] "10.1126/science.1212540"        "10.9745/ghsp-d-21-00145"        #> [5] "10.1126/science.adk9900"        "10.1016/j.ecolecon.2021.107082"  if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ # Compute citation ratio ---- fp_compute_citation_ratio(doi_list) #> $summary #>                                            metric value #> 1                                Total references    38 #> 2                             References with DOI    33 #> 3                         Deduplicated references    33 #> 4                    References found in OpenAlex    33 #> 5                      References found in DAFNEE    11 #> 6     Non-profit and academic friendly references     9 #> 7     For-profit and academic friendly references     2 #> 8 For-profit and non-academic friendly references     0 #> #> $ratios #>     Non-profit and academic friendly     For-profit and academic friendly #>                                 0.82                                 0.18 #> For-profit and non-academic friendly #>                                 0.00 } # }

what: Extract DOI from a BibTeX file or a string — fp_extract_doi
title: Extract DOI from a BibTeX file or a string — fp_extract_doi
text: function detects extracts DOI bibliographic records. User can provides either character vector (argument x) path BibTex file (argument file).

title: Extract DOI from a BibTeX file or a string — fp_extract_doi
code: fp_extract_doi(x = NULL, file = NULL)

title: Extract DOI from a BibTeX file or a string — fp_extract_doi
text: x character vector. string containing bibliographic records. file character length 1. path BibTeX file open.

title: Extract DOI from a BibTeX file or a string — fp_extract_doi
text: character vector extracted DOI. values can NA case books, chapters, etc. references malformed BibTeX.

title: Extract DOI from a BibTeX file or a string — fp_extract_doi

what: Get the fairness status of an article — fp_get_article_fairness
title: Get the fairness status of an article — fp_get_article_fairness
text: querying OpenAlex bibliographic database (https://openalex.org) DAFNEE database (https://dafnee.isem-evolution.fr/), function returns business model academic friendly status article (precisely fairness status journal).

title: Get the fairness status of an article — fp_get_article_fairness
code: fp_get_article_fairness(doi = NULL)

title: Get the fairness status of an article — fp_get_article_fairness
text: doi character length 1. Digital Object Identifiers (DOI) article.

title: Get the fairness status of an article — fp_get_article_fairness
text: data.frame two columns: journal, journal name, fairness, fairness status following possible values: Non-profit academic friendly -profit academic friendly -profit non academic friendly Record found OpenAlex Record found DAFNEE database

title: Get the fairness status of an article — fp_get_article_fairness
code: # Be polite and send your email to OpenAlex API ---- options(openalexR.mailto = 'anonymous@mail.com')  if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ # Fairness status ---- fp_get_article_fairness(doi = "10.1126/science.162.3859.1243") #>   journal                           fairness #> 1 Science   Non-profit and academic friendly  fp_get_article_fairness(doi = "10.1111/j.1461-0248.2005.00792.x") #>           journal                         fairness #> 1 Ecology Letters For-profit and academic friendly  fp_get_article_fairness(doi = "10.1038/35002501") #>   journal                               fairness #> 1  Nature   For-profit and non-academic friendly  # Article not found in OA ---- fp_get_article_fairness(doi = "10.xxxx/xxxx") #>   journal                       fairness #> 1      NA   Record not found in OpenAlex  # Journal not found in the DAFNEE database ---- fp_get_article_fairness(doi = "10.21105/joss.05753") #>                               journal                            fairness #> 1 The Journal of Open Source Software Record not found in DAFNEE database } # }

what: Get the fairness status of a journal — fp_get_journal_fairness
title: Get the fairness status of a journal — fp_get_journal_fairness
text: querying OpenAlex bibliographic database (https://openalex.org) DAFNEE database (https://dafnee.isem-evolution.fr/), function returns business model academic friendly status journal.

title: Get the fairness status of a journal — fp_get_journal_fairness
code: fp_get_journal_fairness(journal = NULL)

title: Get the fairness status of a journal — fp_get_journal_fairness
text: journal character length 1. name journal. use journal abbreviation.

title: Get the fairness status of a journal — fp_get_journal_fairness
text: data.frame two columns: journal, journal name, fairness, fairness status following possible values: Non-profit academic friendly -profit academic friendly -profit non academic friendly Record found OpenAlex Record found DAFNEE database

title: Get the fairness status of a journal — fp_get_journal_fairness
code: # Be polite and send your email to OpenAlex API ---- options(openalexR.mailto = 'anonymous@mail.com')  if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ # Fairness status ---- fp_get_journal_fairness("Science") #>   journal                           fairness #> 1 Science   Non-profit and academic friendly  # Fuzzy search ---- fp_get_journal_fairness("Science of Nature") #> No exact match found! #> The fuzzy search returns these three best candidates: #>   'The Science of Nature' #>   'Science Advances' #>   'People and Nature'  fp_get_journal_fairness("The Science of Nature") #>                 journal                               fairness #> 1 The Science of Nature   For-profit and non-academic friendly } # }

title: Get OpenAlex author ID — fp_get_openalex_author_id
text: Queries OpenAlex bibliographic database (https://openalex.org) retrieve author's identifier.

title: Get OpenAlex author ID — fp_get_openalex_author_id
code: fp_get_openalex_author_id(author = NULL, n = 10)

title: Get OpenAlex author ID — fp_get_openalex_author_id
text: author character vector length 1. Name author. n integer length 1. Number results return (1 200, default 10).

title: Get OpenAlex author ID — fp_get_openalex_author_id
text: data frame following columns: id OpenAlex author ID display_name Author name OpenAlex orcid ORCID identifier works_count Number publications

title: Get OpenAlex author ID — fp_get_openalex_author_id
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ # Be polite and send your email to OpenAlex API ---- options(openalexR.mailto = 'anonymous@mail.com')  fp_get_openalex_author_id("Nicolas Casajus") #>            id    display_name               orcid works_count #> 1 A5004806463 Nicolas Casajus 0000-0002-5537-5294         102  fp_get_openalex_author_id("Nicolas Mouquet") #>            id    display_name               orcid works_count #> 1 A5001034207 Nicolas Mouquet 0000-0003-1840-6984         210 } # }

what: Get and filter an author's works from OpenAlex — fp_get_openalex_author_works
title: Get and filter an author's works from OpenAlex — fp_get_openalex_author_works
text: Queries OpenAlex bibliographic database (https://openalex.org) retrieve works associated OpenAlex author identifier. Optionally filters publication types incomplete records.

title: Get and filter an author's works from OpenAlex — fp_get_openalex_author_works
code: fp_get_openalex_author_works(   author_id = NULL,   select = c("article", "review", "letter"),   drop_na = TRUE )

title: Get and filter an author's works from OpenAlex — fp_get_openalex_author_works
text: author_id character length 1. OpenAlex author ID. identifier can retrieved fp_get_openalex_author_id(). select character vector work types retain. Use fp_list_openalex_work_types() list valid work types. Defaults c("article", "review", "letter"). Set NULL keep work types. drop_na logical. TRUE (default), works missing DOI missing source information removed.

title: Get and filter an author's works from OpenAlex — fp_get_openalex_author_works
text: data frame containing one row per work following columns: id OpenAlex work identifier authors Work (first) author title Work title publication_year Year publication source_display_name Journal source name source_id OpenAlex source identifier doi Digital Object Identifier cited_by_count Citation count OpenAlex type OpenAlex work type

title: Get and filter an author's works from OpenAlex — fp_get_openalex_author_works
text: function wrapper around OpenAlex API using openalexR package. Results automatically standardized cleaned downstream bibliometric analyses. repositories preprint servers (e.g. Zenodo, HAL, bioRxiv, figshare) may excluded depending selected work types.

title: Get and filter an author's works from OpenAlex — fp_get_openalex_author_works
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ # Be polite and send your email to OpenAlex API ---- options(openalexR.mailto = 'anonymous@mail.com')  fp_get_openalex_author_works("A5004806463") #>          id                    authors                               title #> 1 W7143431770  Brunno F. Oliveira et al.     Species range shifts often... #> 2 W7153879999         Miriam Beck et al.    Citation self-awareness for... #> 3 W4406766122 Érica Rievrs Borges et al.         Road‐river intersectio... #> 4 W4415048605   Jonathan Bonfanti et al. Geographic, taxonomic and metr... #> 5 W4411408576      Matthew McLean et al.       Conserving the beauty of... #> 6 W4415113473     Nicolas Casajus et al.           forcis: An R package... #>   publication_year                             source_display_name #> 1             2026 Proceedings of the National Academy of Sciences #> 2             2026                                      BioScience #> 3             2025                      Applied Vegetation Science #> 4             2025                                 Ecology Letters #> 5             2025 Proceedings of the National Academy of Sciences #> 6             2025             The Journal of Open Source Software #>     source_id                     doi cited_by_count    type #> 1  S125754415 10.1073/pnas.2515903123              1 article #> 2  S121830084  10.1093/biosci/biag028              0 article #> 3  S179963793      10.1111/avsc.70011              0 article #> 4   S80967739       10.1111/ele.70220              1  review #> 5  S125754415 10.1073/pnas.2415931122              1 article #> 6 S4210214273     10.21105/joss.09217              0 article } # }

title: Get OpenAlex publication DOI — fp_get_openalex_doi
text: Queries OpenAlex bibliographic database (https://openalex.org) retrieve metadata publications matching given title, including DOI.

title: Get OpenAlex publication DOI — fp_get_openalex_doi
code: fp_get_openalex_doi(title = NULL, n = 10)

title: Get OpenAlex publication DOI — fp_get_openalex_doi
text: title character vector length 1. title publication. n integer length 1. Number results return (1 200, default 10).

title: Get OpenAlex publication DOI — fp_get_openalex_doi
text: data frame following columns: display_name Title publication publication_year Year publication source_display_name Journal source name doi Digital Object Identifier (DOI)

title: Get OpenAlex publication DOI — fp_get_openalex_doi
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ # Be polite and send your email to OpenAlex API ---- options(openalexR.mailto = 'anonymous@mail.com')  # Search for an full title ---- fp_get_openalex_doi(   "Citation self-awareness for a fairer academic publishing landscape" ) #>                                       display_name publication_year #> 1 Citation self-awareness for a fairer academic...             2026 #>   source_display_name                    doi #> 1          BioScience 10.1093/biosci/biag028  # Search for a partial title ---- fp_get_openalex_doi("Citation fairer academic landscape") #>                                       display_name publication_year #> 1     Strategic citations for a fairer academic...             2025 #> 2 Citation self-awareness for a fairer academic...             2026 #>                       source_display_name                       doi #> 1 bioRxiv (Cold Spring Harbor Laboratory) 10.1101/2025.08.06.668908 #> 2                              BioScience    10.1093/biosci/biag028 } # }

what: Identify duplicate works based on title similarity — fp_identify_duplicate_works
title: Identify duplicate works based on title similarity — fp_identify_duplicate_works
text: Groups potentially duplicate bibliographic records computing pairwise string distances work titles clustering similar items.

title: Identify duplicate works based on title similarity — fp_identify_duplicate_works
code: fp_identify_duplicate_works(   data = NULL,   string_dist = "lv",   hclust_method = "single",   threshold = 0.2 )

title: Identify duplicate works based on title similarity — fp_identify_duplicate_works
text: data data.frame containing least title column. string_dist character string specifying distance metric used stringdist::stringdistmatrix(). Defaults "lv" (Levenshtein distance). hclust_method character string specifying hierarchical clustering method used stats::hclust(). Defaults "single". threshold numeric value controlling cluster separation. Lower values produce fine-grained clusters (stricter matching), higher values merge records group.

title: Identify duplicate works based on title similarity — fp_identify_duplicate_works
text: input data.frame additional column: ref_id Integer cluster identifier grouping similar titles.

title: Identify duplicate works based on title similarity — fp_identify_duplicate_works
text: Title similarity computed basic text normalization (lowercasing, punctuation removal, whitespace trimming). Distances calculated using stringdist::stringdistmatrix() normalized title length hierarchical clustering. function remove duplicates assigns cluster identifier can used downstream deduplication grouping.

title: Identify duplicate works based on title similarity — fp_identify_duplicate_works
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ df <- data.frame(   title = c(     "Deep Learning for NLP",     "Deep learning for natural language processing",     "Quantum Computing Basics"   ) )  fp_identify_duplicate_works(df) } # }

title: List DAFNEE journals — fp_list_dafnee_journals
text: DAFNEE database (Database Academia‑Friendly Journals Ecology Evolution, https://dafnee.isem-evolution.fr/) provides business model academic friendly status several journals field Ecology Evolution. fairpub package provides selection 287 DAFNEE journals function returns information journals.

title: List DAFNEE journals — fp_list_dafnee_journals
code: fp_list_dafnee_journals()

title: List DAFNEE journals — fp_list_dafnee_journals
text: data.frame three columns: journal, name journal business_model, business model journal (non-profit -profit) academic_friendly, academic friendly status journal (yes )

title: List DAFNEE journals — fp_list_dafnee_journals
code: # List DAFNEE journals in fairpub ---- journals <- fp_list_dafnee_journals()  # Number of journals ---- nrow(journals) #> [1] 287  # Preview of the outputs ---- head(journals) #>                                                           journal #> 1                                                  Acta Amazonica #> 2                                                 acta ethologica #> 3                                                 Acta Oecologica #> 4 Advances in ecological research/Advances in Ecological Research #> 5                                      African Journal of Ecology #> 6                     African Journal of Range and Forage Science #>   business_model academic_friendly #> 1     Non-profit               Yes #> 2     For-profit                No #> 3     For-profit                No #> 4     For-profit                No #> 5     For-profit               Yes #> 6     For-profit               Yes
